disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute severe physical collapse and profound generalised weakness came on within hours of extreme physical over-exertion, in a person with a chronic illness that causes post-exertional crashes
trigger_question_examples: What happened just before you collapsed?
What were you doing today before this started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I really overdid it physically today trying to keep up with everyone, and a few hours later I just crashed — I've never felt this wiped out and weak before.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reports a racing, pounding heart and a home/wearable heart-rate reading around 108
trigger_question_examples: How is your heart — is it racing or pounding?
Have you been able to check your pulse or heart rate?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My heart's been pounding and racing since it happened — my watch says it's around 108.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feels very lightheaded and faint on sitting or standing up, with a low home blood-pressure reading
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel faint or dizzy when you sit up or stand?
Have you managed to take your blood pressure at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Whenever I try to sit up I go really lightheaded like I'll pass out, and our home monitor said my blood pressure was 95 over 60.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, chest pressure or shortness of breath reported at present
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any chest pain, pressure or tightness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't have chest pain and I'm not really short of breath — it's the weakness and the racing heart that scare me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No vomiting, no severe abdominal pain, and no confusion; alert and oriented
trigger_question_examples: Any vomiting or bad tummy pain?
Do you feel confused, or is your thinking clear?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No vomiting or bad stomach pain, and my head's clear — I just feel utterly drained and weak.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound weakness with inability to stand or walk safely; high falls risk, and a mobility aid is available but the patient has been reluctant to use it
trigger_question_examples: Can you stand or walk safely right now?
Do you have anything to help you move around safely at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't stand or walk safely at all right now — I'm bedbound. I do have a walking frame but honestly I've been avoiding using it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Longstanding grief and distress about lost physical capacity, social isolation from former friend groups, and feelings of low self-worth
trigger_question_examples: How have you been coping emotionally with your health?
It sounds like this has been really hard — can you tell me more about how you've been feeling?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I grieve the active person I used to be, I feel cut off from my old friends, and some days I question what I'm even worth. That's why I pushed so hard today.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new weakness or numbness confined to one side of the body, no facial droop and no slurred speech
trigger_question_examples: Is the weakness worse on one side than the other?
Any drooping of your face or trouble speaking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's my whole body evenly — not one side — and my face and speech are fine, I'm just exhausted.
